translations.> NSAttributedString attributes: [NSAttr: > NSAttributedString attributes: [NSAttributedString.Key: Any?] now consider Equatable when merging Range Attributes in iOS ≥ 18, which is different from before.
NSAttributedString attributes will **automatically compare adjacent Range Attributes with the same .key to see if they are identical, and merge them into a single Attribute if they are**. For example:
translations.NSAttributedString attributes: [NSAttrib: NSAttributedString attributes: [NSAttributedString.Key: Any?] now consider Equatable when merging Range Attributes in iOS ≥ 18, which is different from before.
translations.Trace Code 後定位到閃退問題點是遍歷 `.breaklinePlace: After tracing the code, the crash point is located when iterating over `.breaklinePlaceholder` attributes and performing deletion on the Range:
translations.`.breaklinePlaceholder` 是我自行擴充的一個 NSAttr: `.breaklinePlaceholder` is a custom NSAttributedString.Key I created to mark HTML tag information and optimize the use of line break characters:

```swift
NSRange {0, 13}: <UICTFont: 0x101d13400> font-family: ".SFUI-Regular"; font-weight: normal; font-style: normal; font-size: 14.00pt
NSRange {13, 4}: <UICTFont: 0x101d13860> font-family: ".SFUI-Regular"; font-weight: normal; font-style: normal; font-size: 12.00pt
```

translations.推測底層是使用 `Set<Hashable>` 做為 Attributes 容器: It is inferred that the underlying implementation uses `Set<Hashable>` as the container for Attributes, which automatically excludes duplicate Attribute objects.

translations.比較 iOS 17/18 有無實現 Equatable/Hashable 的結果: Comparison of iOS 17/18 Results with or without Implementing Equatable/Hashable
translations.看到這個 Issue 有點困惑，因為程式在以前都沒問題，iOS 18 開始才會閃: Seeing this issue is a bit confusing because the code worked fine before, and it only crashes starting with iOS 18. This is unusual and likely caused by some underlying changes in Foundation in iOS 18.
translations.結合前述， `NSAttributedString attributes: [N: Combining the above, the `NSAttributedString attributes: [NSAttributedString.Key: Any?]` value is declared as type `Any?`. **Based on observations, iOS ≥ 18 first checks equality using `Equatable`, then uses a `Hashable` set to merge and manage objects.**
